title: reholttumia papuana
intro: Reholttumia papuana, a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, is a shrub or small tree native to Papua New Guinea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Reholttumia papuana include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Reholttumia papuana include infusions and decoctions, which are typically made from the leaves or bark of the plant, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
